Label: Moderate Institutional Trust
Description: This class shows a tendency toward moderate trust (rating 4) across most institutions, with notably higher trust in the president and armed forces, and lower trust in the police and political parties.

Label: Maximum Institutional Trust
Description: This class is characterized by very high probabilities of reporting maximum trust (7) across nearly all institutions, most notably the president (0.82), public ministry (0.74), and armed forces (0.72).

Label: High Institutional Trust
Description: This class is characterized by high levels of trust across all institutions, with the strongest probabilities concentrated on ratings of 5, 6, or 7, particularly for the president, armed forces, and supreme court.

Label: Generalized Institutional Distrust
Description: This class is characterized by very high probabilities of reporting the lowest level of trust (1) across nearly all institutions, most notably for political parties (85%) and the police (76%).

Label: Presidential and Military Trust
Description: This class is characterized by high distrust of political parties and police, while showing relatively higher trust in the president and armed forces compared to other institutions.
